name: Backstein, Rytha Ferdinand
description: Rytha started out well with the Class of 1915, but found that she couldn't keep up with 1915—so by attending summer school she graduates with the Class of 1914. Three years for college with honors is doing pretty well.
text: Rytha started out well with the Class of 1915, but found that she couldn't keep up with 1915—so by attending summer school she graduates with the Class of 1914. Three years for college with honors is doing pretty well.

name: Bradstreet, Herbert Neal
description: "Brad" has made college in three years—no small stunt indeed. "Brad" came back to us this year after two years following his profession. We wish you success as an engineer and know you will succeed.
text: "Brad" has made college in three years—no small stunt indeed. "Brad" came back to us this year after two years following his profession. We wish you success as an engineer and know you will succeed.

name: Merritt, Richard
description: "Dick" is the last of the original "Prescott Colony" that have become famous in the annals of the school. "Dick" has taken an active interest in football, baseball and all college activities during his residence with us. We hope that he will be as successful as an engineer as he has been in college.
text: "Dick" is the last of the original "Prescott Colony" that have become famous in the annals of the school. "Dick" has taken an active interest in football, baseball and all college activities during his residence with us. We hope that he will be as successful as an engineer as he has been in college.

name: Mcpherson, Maud
description: For three long years Maud hid herself behind her books, not venturing forth for a "How-di-do" except on Sundays. But behold Maud in her Senior year. She carried Class Honors in her Sophomore, Junior, and Senior years as well as University Scholar. Some record, and her Senior year she took an active part in all scholastic activities.
text: For three long years Maud hid herself behind her books, not venturing forth for a "How-di-do" except on Sundays. But behold Maud in her Senior year. She carried Class Honors in her Sophomore, Junior, and Senior years as well as University Scholar. Some record, and her Senior year she took an active part in all scholastic activities.

name: Alfred, D. Micotti
description: "Mac" laid low till his last year and then blossomed forth as Editor of "Life", the most thankless job. Good work "Mac" to stay with it. One of the three to graduate in Civil Engineering, all of which will make good.
text: "Mac" laid low till his last year and then blossomed forth as Editor of "Life", the most thankless job. Good work "Mac" to stay with it. One of the three to graduate in Civil Engineering, all of which will make good.

name: Wooddell, Grace Helen
description: In all school activities Grace's smiling face has been seen—for she is an ever willing and hard worker. As social manager, she has no equal. Good-bye Grace, we hate to see you go. P.S.—Although not Dutch she is very fond of (Saur) kraut.
text: In all school activities Grace's smiling face has been seen—for she is an ever willing and hard worker. As social manager, she has no equal. Good-bye Grace, we hate to see you go. P.S.—Although not Dutch she is very fond of (Saur) kraut.

name: Rogers, Frederick William
description: Fred has been honored by passing his examinations and being selected as Rhodes Scholar, Oxford College, England. Some class eh! Fred expects to be a Physics shark some day.
text: Fred has been honored by passing his examinations and being selected as Rhodes Scholar, Oxford College, England. Some class eh! Fred expects to be a Physics shark some day.

name: Abbott, Frank Reuben
motto: "Providence, R. I. When one meets a man who claims to be wholly without vice, you will have to get out a searchlight in order to find his virtues."
text: "Providence, R. I. When one meets a man who claims to be wholly without vice, you will have to get out a searchlight in order to find his virtues."

name: Barkley, Bessie Jewell
motto: "Tucson, Arizona. Few lips would be moved to kissing if they could find a sufficiency of singing."
text: "Tucson, Arizona. Few lips would be moved to kissing if they could find a sufficiency of singing."

name: Brewer, Walter Martin
motto: "Los Angeles, Cal. Too much work and too much energy kills a man just as effectively as too much assorted vice or too much drink."
text: "Los Angeles, Cal. Too much work and too much energy kills a man just as effectively as too much assorted vice or too much drink."

name: Culin, Frank Lewis Jr.
motto: "Man's home has no past tense—woman's no future".
text: "Man's home has no past tense—woman's no future".

name: Carter, Mabel Ruth
motto: "Never show a woman that you care a snap of a finger for her and begad she will come bleatin' to your footheels".
text: "Never show a woman that you care a snap of a finger for her and begad she will come bleatin' to your footheels".

name: Gameros, Luis Guillermo
motto: "I never see'd the ale I could not drink, the 'bacco I could not smoke, nor the lass I could not kiss".
text: "I never see'd the ale I could not drink, the 'bacco I could not smoke, nor the lass I could not kiss".

name: Goyette, Charles Edgar
motto: "Surely you ought to know that the first proof a man gives of his interest in a woman is by talking to her about his own sweet self. If the woman listens without yawning, he begins to like her; if she flatters the animal vanity, he ends by adoring her. A great idea struck him—matrimony."
text: "Surely you ought to know that the first proof a man gives of his interest in a woman is by talking to her about his own sweet self. If the woman listens without yawning, he begins to like her; if she flatters the animal vanity, he ends by adoring her. A great idea struck him—matrimony."

name: Curry, Esther May
motto: "Take my word for it, any woman can manage a clever man, but it needs a clever woman to manage a fool."
text: "Take my word for it, any woman can manage a clever man, but it needs a clever woman to manage a fool."

name: Hayhurst, Normal Clifton
motto: "...........and did much good work and was honored by all who knew him."
text: "...........and did much good work and was honored by all who knew him."

name: Jackson, Lawrence Richard
motto: "We walked about saying nothing—because we were friends and talking spoils good tobacco."
text: "We walked about saying nothing—because we were friends and talking spoils good tobacco."

name: Duffy, Catherine Gertrude
motto: "I am sometimes sorry that I am a woman. But I am very glad that I am not a man, and—I shouldn't care to be an angel."
text: "I am sometimes sorry that I am a woman. But I am very glad that I am not a man, and—I shouldn't care to be an angel."

name: LaTourette, Verne Gerald
motto: "I may be introduced to the other world against my will, but I know my duty to this, as long as I stay in it."
text: "I may be introduced to the other world against my will, but I know my duty to this, as long as I stay in it."

name: Franklin, Alfred Luis Mining
motto: "Alone with himself, walking, wondering and thinking, his eyes on the ground and his thoughts with the clouds."
text: "Alone with himself, walking, wondering and thinking, his eyes on the ground and his thoughts with the clouds."

name: Lawson, Alice Patton
motto: "When a man does good work out of all proportion to his income, in seven cases out of ten there is a woman at the back of his virtue."
text: "When a man does good work out of all proportion to his income, in seven cases out of ten there is a woman at the back of his virtue."

name: Lynch, Eugene Read
motto: "A marvelous capacity for making bad puns."
text: "A marvelous capacity for making bad puns."

name: Mack, Francis Cagwin
motto: "I have lived long enough to know that it is best to know nothing."
text: "I have lived long enough to know that it is best to know nothing."

name: Rockfellow, Julia
motto: "Men and women of the noblest dispositions think themselves happiest when others share their happiness with them. - Her words are bonds, her oaths are oracles, her love sincere, her thoughts immaculate."
text: "Men and women of the noblest dispositions think themselves happiest when others share their happiness with them. - Her words are bonds, her oaths are oracles, her love sincere, her thoughts immaculate."

name: McIntosh, Jay Angus
motto: "He is such an aggressive—cock sure, you be damned fellow, but he'll get that knocked out of him in time."
text: "He is such an aggressive—cock sure, you be damned fellow, but he'll get that knocked out of him in time."

name: Percy, Frank
motto: "No one has yet explained what actually happens when an irresistible force meets the immovable post—though some have thought deeply."
text: "No one has yet explained what actually happens when an irresistible force meets the immovable post—though some have thought deeply."

name: Oxley, Edward Burton
motto: "When a woman is very, very bad she is awful, but when a man is correspondingly good he is weird."
text: "When a woman is very, very bad she is awful, but when a man is correspondingly good he is weird."

name: Rigg, Ralph Lee
motto: "Never, never tell your wife anything that you do not wish her to remember, and think over all her life."
text: "Never, never tell your wife anything that you do not wish her to remember, and think over all her life."
